avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase but remain within the current danger level.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of sharply rising temperatures and rain up to high altitudes a treacherous avalanche situation will develop. The rain will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack in all aspects. These conditions will cause a rapid weakening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind, wind slabs will form in particular in high Alpine regions. On very steep slopes the moist avalanches can be released naturally and reach medium size in isolated cases. As a consequence of warming there will be an increase in the avalanche danger.
Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Avalanches can be released, mostly by large loads and reach medium size. In very isolated cases these can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2800 m. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. Some snow will fall in some localities. This applies above approximately 2400 m. Some rain will fall over a wide area.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 2 to 5 cm of snow fell in the past few hours above approximately 2500 m. Today the weather will be cloudy. Above approximately 2500 m: Some snow will fall today in some localities.

These weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects between approximately 2100 and 2700 m. As the penetration by moisture increases moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they can reach medium size in isolated cases. This applies especially on steep slopes at high altitude, and on very steep west, north and east facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated during the last few days.

An overcast night: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes. Low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack is hardly frozen at all.

The rain will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack above approximately 2600 m.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In some localities 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 3000 m. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions 5 cm of snow, and up to 10 cm in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 2500 m.
Today the weather will be cloudy. Some snow will fall until the evening in some localities. Up to 2300 m rain will fall.

The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. The new snow is bonding quite well with the old snowpack. Individual mostly small natural avalanches are, however, possible.

The high humditiy will give rise from the early morning to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects in particular at intermediate altitudes. As the penetration by moisture increases moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they can reach medium size in isolated cases.

In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation and on steep slopes the avalanche danger is a little higher.

snowpackStructure.comment:
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is moist and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a crust that is barely capable of bearing a load. In the regions exposed to snowfall this applies.

Below approximately 2500 m: The snowpack is moist. In addition, only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: Slight increase in danger of moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially below approximately 2600 m.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, clearly visible wind slabs will form in particular in high Alpine regions. These are small and can only be released in isolated cases.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads in particular on very steep shady slopes, in particular above approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted at times. Some rain will fall until late morning in some regions.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions generally. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 2600 m rain fell yesterday. In the early morning the weather will be cloudy. Up to 2500 m rain will fall from midday in some localities. The backcountry touring conditions are unfavourable.

The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects in all altitude zones.

As the penetration by moisture increases natural moist avalanches are possible at any time, but they can reach medium size in isolated cases. This applies especially on steep slopes at high altitude, and on very steep west, north and east facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2500 m: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated during the last few days. The surface of the snowpack cooled hardly at all during the overcast night and will soften quickly.

Below approximately 2500 m: The rain gave rise to extreme moistening of the snowpack over a wide area. In addition, only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Light precipitation: The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and in gullies and bowls. Individual wet and gliding avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover is absent or patchy in many areas, especially on southern slopes. Due to the temperatures, the snowpack is steadily decreasing. The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. In very isolated cases weak layers exist in the snowpack on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be clear. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good.
Decrease in avalanche danger as the temperature drops.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality.
The sleet gave rise on Tuesday to significant moistening of the snowpack in all aspects below approximately 2700 m.
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. The weather will be cloudy.
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. More wet snow slides and avalanches are to be expected, even medium-sized ones, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep north facing slopes below approximately 2600 m, and on very steep east, south and west facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 2500 m rain will fall in the next few hours.
Wednesday: Some rain will fall from midday.
In the last few days the weather was mild. This situation gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes.
Hardly any more avalanches were reported.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
Especially on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m no snow is lying. On shady slopes below the tree line hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Some rain will fall. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially at high altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls at high altitude there is still a very large amount of snow. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will soften quickly. Some rain will fall in the afternoon.
Only a little snow is now lying.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place individual wet avalanches are possible. Mostly they are rather small.
Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: Some rain will fall in some regions. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be barely evident over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack is not frozen and will soften quickly.
